signals.who: buyer (Florence Tinsley-Roy CPO/EB, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood, Praveen Seemundun)
signals.evidence: Dear Florence, Praveen, Stephen, Paras, Thank you very much for the constructive call today... we will come back to you by tomorrow with our OF2 / SoW
Florence CPO hired one of our senior consultant who will be PM for her... She wants to sign before end of July (She is in vacation in 2 weeks)
Please find attached our revised document... clean everything that we accepted... to get closer to executable version
Completed: NDA Publicis Groupe Holdings B.V._(2023) and Zycus Infotech B.V. — All parties finished
Please find attached the document completed on the functional part... Do you have availability on Friday or Monday to discuss it?
Can we please move to 11am Tuesday or Wednesday? Thanks Florence
sent 3 reminders asking them to schedule a WS... 0 Feedback -> both present on the 8 Jul constructive call
MEDDPICC 2.0: 'No competition'; no rival named in any source 11 May-8 Jul

top_rationale: Vendor Selected + Commit, sole-sourced: Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO) is the economic buyer, champion and decision-maker; MEDDPICC records the decision as already made and no competitor appears anywhere.

The deal is functionally at Contract/Negotiation, not Vendor Selected: BAFO sent 24 Jun, MSA legal clauses resolved and NDA executed 29 Jun, buyer returned redlines 26 Jun, SoW/OF1/OF2 in final review as of 8 Jul.
Strongest signal is champion investment: Florence hired the project's incoming PM (joins end-Aug) ahead of an unsigned contract and is driving the signature timeline around her own vacation.

Momentum is accelerating: the 8 Jul EB call finally pulled in the previously-stalled process validators (Stephen Cook, Paras Sood), with a stack of near-term dated milestones (9-10 Jul commercial call + Merlin demo, 13 Jul Merlin Review, 24 Jul finalization).

Only Avoma entry is a Sep-2025 dinner event (>90d, zero weight); all current-cycle facts come from the three gold-mine sources (Next Step, Next Step History, completed Task email bodies), which are complete.

Main risks are timing and thread-depth: close date pushed 15 days to 30 Jul, Florence on vacation ~2 weeks out, single-threaded on Florence for exec, and an open ~50K Merlin core-vs-option commercial point.

drivers: ✅ Sole-source selection at Vendor Selected + Commit forecast — Florence (CPO/EB) has decided (MEDDPICC 'decision already done'), no competitor named in any source since 11 May.
✅ Economic buyer IS the champion and is investing ahead of signature — Florence hired the project's incoming PM and personally set the signing deadline around her own vacation.
✅ Contract mechanics in final review — BAFO 24 Jun, MSA legal clauses resolved + NDA executed 29 Jun, buyer redlines returned 26 Jun, full package (MSA/OF1/SoW) with buyer as of 8 Jul.

⚠️ Close date pushed 15 days (15→30 Jul on 8 Jul) with Florence heading into a ~2-week vacation — signing window compressed. ► Lock a firm signature date and confirm a signing delegate/authority if she is out; hold the 9-10 Jul calls.

⚠️ Process-validators (Stephen Cook, Paras Sood) only re-engaged on 8 Jul after stalling since 12 Jun — technical/process sign-off not fully closed. ► Get explicit 'no blockers' from Stephen/Paras on the 9-10 Jul commercial + demo calls.

⚠️ Business case unquantified and ~50K TCV swing open (Merlin for iContract core-vs-option). ► Quantify Florence's ROI for the year-end mandate and resolve the Merlin core-vs-option point before finalization.
win.focus_now: Convert a sole-source selection into a signed contract before Florence's vacation — lock the signing date, close Stephen/Paras process sign-off on the 9-10 Jul calls, and settle the Merlin core-vs-option commercial point.
win.ceiling_applied: Stage ceiling 85 crossed — §5.1 permits it (ForecastCategory=Commit AND stage ≥ Vendor Selected) and §5.5 stage-reality up-override applies (true stage Contract/Negotiation).
win.exception_statement: Recorded StageName = Vendor Selected (anchor 72, cap 85), but hard paper evidence — BAFO sent 24 Jun 2026, MSA legal clauses resolved and NDA executed 29 Jun 2026, buyer returned contract redlines 26 Jun 2026, and SoW/OF1/OF2 in final review as of 8 Jul 2026 — shows the deal operating at Contract/Negotiation, scored against that higher anchor with Commit forecast. ► Advance StageName to Contract/Negotiation in Salesforce to match reality.

drivers: ✅ EB-level working call on 8 Jul that finally pulled in the stalled process validators — Florence (CPO), Praveen, Stephen Cook and Paras Sood on one 'constructive call'; full SoW/contract package issued the same day.
✅ Dense two-way buyer deliverables in the last two weeks — buyer returned contract redlines (26 Jun), executed the NDA (29 Jun) and sent the completed functional document (1 Jul).

✅ Fresh, advancing Next Step (8 Jul, 90% confidence) with near-term dated milestones — 9 Jul commercial call, 9-10 Jul Merlin/iContract demo, 24 Jul contract finalization; 13 Jul Merlin Review already scheduled.
✅ Forecast upgraded to Commit and amount raised 230K→250K; buyer (Émeline) actively driving four OF1 iterations ('super pushy').

⚠️ Close date pushed 15 days (15→30 Jul) with Florence heading into a ~2-week vacation — tempo must convert to signature fast. ► Lock a firm signing date and a signing delegate; keep the 9-10 Jul calls on the calendar.

⚠️ Open commercial point (Merlin for iContract core-vs-option, ~50K TCV) could stall the final paper. ► Resolve the Merlin core-vs-option decision on the 9 Jul commercial call so OF1/SoW can be finalized.
mom.focus_now: Keep the tempo through signature — run the 9-10 Jul commercial + demo calls, resolve the Merlin core-vs-option point, and get the paper signed before Florence's vacation.

best_practices.item: Multi-thread beyond Florence and secure a signing delegate / backup approver before her vacation
best_practices.rank_reason: Single-threaded on Florence for exec authority and she goes on vacation ~22 Jul — the single biggest close risk in a compressed window

best_practices.item: Lock a mutual close/signature plan running through the 30 Jul close date
best_practices.rank_reason: Converts a Commit-forecast, sole-source selection into signed paper inside a tight, vacation-compressed window

best_practices.item: Confirm sole-source / no late competitor entering before signature
best_practices.rank_reason: No competitor is documented in a $250K CPO-led procurement — validate there is no last-minute bake-off before committing

best_practices.item: Quantify the ROI / business case for Florence's year-end procurement-impact mandate
best_practices.rank_reason: Pain is qualitative and unquantified; a hard impact statement de-risks final sign-off and the Merlin-scope decision

sum.headline: Zycus held a constructive close-out call on 8 Jul with CPO Florence Tinsley-Roy and the process validators (Stephen Cook, Paras Sood) and delivered the full contract package (MSA, OF1, SoW) — though the close date was pushed from 15 to 30 Jul.
supporting: Florence has hired Zycus's incoming project manager ahead of signature and wants to sign before her end-of-July vacation; a commercial call (9 Jul) and Merlin-for-iContract demo (10 Jul) are set, with contract finalization targeted for 24 Jul.
The open commercial point is whether Merlin for iContract stays a core module or moves to an option (~50K TCV swing).
sum.as_of_note: Reflects activity through the 8 Jul 2026 last-business-day window (today 9 Jul 2026).
